howItWorks.intro: This ideal weight calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Which ideal weight formula is best?
faq.answer: No single formula is perfect. We show multiple formulas so you can see the range. The BMI-based range (18.5-24.9) is the most widely used clinical standard.

faq.question: Does frame size matter?
faq.answer: Yes. Large-framed individuals naturally weigh more. Measure your wrist: under 6.5 inches is small frame, 6.5-7.5 is medium, over 7.5 is large for men.

faq.question: Is ideal weight the same as healthiest weight?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Health depends on body composition, fitness level, and metabolic markers — not just scale weight. A fit person at a higher weight can be healthier than a sedentary person at ideal weight.

faq.question: How much variation is normal?
faq.answer: The formulas vary by 10-15 lbs for the same height. Your personal ideal depends on muscle mass, body frame, age, and how you feel.
